first_line_drugs.notes: Neonates: 2 ml/kg IV bolus (200 mg/kg), then start infusion
Children: 2–4 ml/kg IV bolus (max D25 preferred), repeat if needed after 5–10 min
Start GIR 4–6 mg/kg/min, titrate up to 8–12 mg/kg/min (up to 20 mg/kg/min in hyperinsulinism)

second_line_drugs.notes: 0.03–0.1 mg/kg IM/IV (max 1 mg) in refractory hypoglycemia or no IV access
2–5 mg/kg IV if adrenal insufficiency suspected

counselling: Child needs urgent glucose correction and monitoring — बच्चे को तुरंत शुगर (ग्लूकोज़) ठीक करना और निगरानी ज़रूरी है।
Ensure frequent feeding after stabilization — स्थिति ठीक होने के बाद बार-बार थोड़ा-थोड़ा दूध/खाना दें।
Avoid prolonged fasting — बच्चे को लंबे समय तक भूखा न रखें।
Give medicines as advised strictly — दवाइयाँ समय पर और बताई गई मात्रा में दें।
Follow-up for cause evaluation is important — कारण की जाँच के लिए फॉलो-अप ज़रूरी है।
referral_criteria: Refer to pediatric intensivist/endocrinologist if persistent or recurrent hypoglycemia
hospitalisation_criteria: Symptomatic hypoglycemia, seizures, need for IV glucose infusion, recurrent episodes
notes: Always treat hypoglycemia immediately before confirming cause. Avoid delays.
source: IAP Guidelines 2024, WHO Emergency Care, Pediatric Endocrine Protocols
guideline_changes: Higher GIR targets recommended in hyperinsulinemic states
expected_course: Rapid improvement within minutes after correction; recurrence depends on underlying cause
key_clinical_points: Check glucose in all sick children
Start treatment immediately without waiting for reports
